claim: The collective ledger can make a bounded distributed continuity claim with signed quorum, disclosed disagreement, dynamic subgroup weighting, and proof-preserved compression.

consensus_policy.signature_requirement: each validator signs the accepted ledger root
consensus_policy.root_comparison: compare reported node roots against accepted_root
consensus_policy.fork_handling: forks are disclosed and excluded from promotion until reconciled
consensus_policy.reason: distributed trust must not depend on one mutable ledger holder

weight_policy.evidence_basis: weights may move only when coverage, external validation, and risk records support the change
weight_policy.risk_adjustment: riskier subgroups are capped until audit recovery
weight_policy.reason: adaptive weights must not hide manipulation or performance overfit

retention_policy.proof_method: inclusion proof retained for pruned records
retention_policy.pruning_rule: only non-exception records older than full_retention_window may be compressed
retention_policy.reason: compression may reduce storage but must not erase auditability

non_claim_locks: proof of continuity is not proof of value
